title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Veterans 4 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CLACKAMAS COUNTY COORDINATED HOUSING ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a centralized access point for households experiencing homelessness or a housing crisis through a collaboration of multiple agencies. Eligible households are reviewed for services and added to the Housing By Name List for all qualifying programs. Assistance with rent or moving costs is limited, not guaranteed, and may depend on factors such as urban growth boundaries, eviction notices, court dates, and funding. Eligibility: Individuals and families who are homeless or at imminent risk of becoming homeless seeking prevention/diversion services to stay in their current homes Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Off-peak call times: Monday-Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (503) 655-8575 Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY VETERAN SERVICES OFFICE LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 1870 W 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies by site Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 344-0465 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in costs and rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 7916 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans and their households, who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income. Households are only eligible if the veteran applies and is eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Walk in: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (855) 425-5544 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS TRANSITION PROJECTS VETERAN SERVICES VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
